rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK VS. HANS KELLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 27, 2012, Line 4. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. The claim of exemption is denied because the claimant has not shown that an exemption applies. Claimant states that the checking account for his business is exempt because the funds belong to his customers for products to be sold. However, the statutes cited only provide exemptions for paid earnings. Claimant has not shown that the funds in the account are paid earnings. Claimant also has not shown that the account is a checking account for his business. To the extent that the account is in claimant's personal name, it is properly levied on. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TOBI.COM, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. CATHERINE CHOW, A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disqualify Joshua Cohen As Counsel For Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 27, 2012, Line 22. PLAINTIFF TOBI.COM, LLC'S Motion To Disqualify Joshua Cohen As Counsel For Defendants. The Court grants the motion to disqualify under Kennedy v. Eldridge (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 1197. The Court finds that there is a significant danger that¿as a result of Mr. Cohen¿s prior attorney-client relationship with Mr. English (Plaintiff¿s former Chairman)¿Mr. Cohen acquired relevant confidential information about Plaintiff to which he otherwise would not have had access. That confidential information is substantially related to the instant dispute, and would likely be used advantageously in this litigation against Plaintiff. For example, Mr. Cohen received Tobi.com board of directors materials, including a draft settlement letter that Tobi.com contemplated sending pertaining to this lawsuit and the same defendants who Mr. Cohen now represents. (English Decl. in Opposition to Motion, ¶3, Ex. A.) Mr. Cohen discussed the substance of the January 2012 Tobi.com board meeting (where this lawsuit was discussed), with his then-client English. English at the time was acting in the capacity of Chairman of Tobi.com. As attorney for English, Mr. Cohen reviewed the draft settlement letter before it was sent to his now clients. (Cohen Decl. in Opposition to Motion, ¶6). Mr. Cohen, though formally acting as attorney for board chair English, went so far as to write an email to Tobi.com counsel commenting on the proposed settlement letter and its effect on Tobi.com. (Id. at ¶7, Ex. A.) with this Mr. Cohen¿s continued representation of defendants in this case undermines the integrity of the judicial process. Kennedy v. Eldridge, supra, 210 Cal. App. 4th at 1205. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party¿s counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
